scores.correctness_notes: The response covers the general approach correctly (fetch-lib, charmcraft.yaml provides endpoint, implement provider class) but misses the key distinction in the gold standard about PyPI packages (charmlibs.interfaces.* namespace) vs Charmhub-hosted libraries. It also doesn't mention the interface libraries listing or Juju Secrets for sensitive data.
scores.hallucination_notes: The 'set_data' method shown in the example may not exist in real libraries — this is a generic placeholder. The 'MyInterfaceProviderEvents', 'MyInterfaceRequestedEvent' pattern is plausible but not verified against actual charmlib APIs. The charmcraft.yaml 'optional: true' field under provides is not standard. The reference to 'charmhub.io/interfaces' may not be the correct URL for the interface libraries listing (gold standard points to documentation.ubuntu.com/charmlibs).
